codeReview.summary: Implementation adds conflict resolution for memory contradictions. New module conflict_resolution.rs (1331 lines) provides MemoryConflictDetector, MemoryConflictResolver, ConflictResolutionService with 19 passing tests. All three expected behaviors implemented.

issues.description: Dead code: ConflictMemoryInfo::outranks method is defined but never called anywhere in the codebase. The resolution strategies use direct comparison logic instead.
Unused field: ConflictResolutionConfig::same_generation_days is set in default() and builder methods but never referenced in determine_conflict_type() or any other function.

MemorySource::outranks doesn't handle all combinations. Unknown outranks nothing and nothing outranks Unknown, which could cause edge cases if Unknown source memories are involved in conflicts. However, this doesn't affect current resolution strategies which bypass outranks.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker followed Rust skill procedure correctly. However, the skill's SKILL.md doesn't have specific guidance on conflict resolution patterns - this was novel implementation work. No gap in skill coverage, just that this feature required custom design beyond documented patterns.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Transcript shows worker explored existing memory crate structure before implementing. skillFeedback.followedProcedure is true.

summary: PASS - Implementation complete. Memory conflict detection and resolution with configurable rules (higher_confidence_wins, newer_wins, operator_overrides, keep_original, keep_new, merge, manual). Repository-scoped conflict detection works correctly. All 19 tests pass. Three minor dead code issues identified but none blocking functionality.
